threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver says 'mind the gap' in a thick Cockney accent. The locals all chuckle at your expression.
Caught a guy in a full panda suit getting on the N133 last night. He just sat there, sipping his coffee, completely unfazed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting at the Kottbusser Tor, I watched a street artist juggle while a guy on a bike shouted for someone to catch his coffee. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a bar serving cocktails named after famous protests, like 'Blockupy Breeze.' It's quirky, even for Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're locals when they dodge the BART Civic Center evacuation chaos like pros, while newcomers just stare in confusion.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the Muni crowd with a ukulele in the Tenderloin last night. He played 'Somewhere Over the Rainbow' while the bus was stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are just for selfies. Locals use them for evening gatherings, exchanging stories over a tinitus or two.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them hesitating at the tram stop, checking their phones while trying to figure out which line goes where—classic rookie move.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'Overground' like it's the only option. Welcome to Dalston, mate. You've got a lot to learn about getting around.
Last night, I overheard two guys arguing about which chicken shop has the best wings, all while waiting for the 196. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a guy fixing his bike with an espresso in one hand and a pet pigeon on his shoulder. Classic Neukölln chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find techno parties in abandoned buildings that start at sunrise. It's like a scene from a movie, but it's just Saturday for us.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they casually recognize the bus driver’s name and exact schedule changes, like it’s part of their routine.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The Muni driver just stopped mid-route to let a pigeon cross the street. Didn't even flinch when the guy behind him shouted. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the 'barrio' is overrun by hipsters, but real locals stick to the quiet corners with family-run bakeries and old-school bodegas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their oversized grocery bags; seasoned locals know to bring a tote for the markets around here.
